body_txt: This is the technique of putting a sarcastic twist on the more plain meaning of something. Though literal-mindedness is often associated with not understanding sarcasm , in some cases snarking and literal-mindedness can go hand-in-hand. Be warned that this tactic has a tendency to backfire, as the recipient might shoot back with calling you dumb for "not understanding figures of speech" or what-have-you — which, in turn, makes them "guilty" of not understanding what you meant. Often involves a Mathematician's Answer . See also Grammar Nazi , whose more snarky varieties are prone to responding to misspoken statements as if they were what the speaker meant to say. If one acts on the sarcastic twist, it's a Jerkass Genie . If the snarky twist is the intended meaning, it may be a form of Literal Metaphor .
